name: zong-wifi

documentation.readme: ## Vulnerabilities and Exploits Although the device use a wierd authentication method, it is nothing more than a gimmick used by the UI code to give an illusion of authentication. All the endpoints are accessible directly without authentication and the best part of it all is you can use the admin endpoint to get the username and password for the router.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with jayofelony/pwnagotchi (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with evilsocket/pwnagotchi (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with coincoin7/Wireless-Router-Vulnerability (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: Zong FiberHome 4G Device.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: ## Vulnerabilities and Exploits Although the device use a wierd authentication method, it is nothing more than a gimmick used by the UI code to give an illusion of authentication. All the endpoints are accessible directly without authentication and the best part of it all is you can use the admin endpoint to get the username and password for the router.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `adb connect $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST:5555`. Further invocations from upstream: `cat /proc/mtd`.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Wireless, use zong-wifi because Zong FiberHome 4G Device.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `adb connect $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST:5555` as documented upstream for zong-wifi.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cat /proc/mtd` as documented upstream for zong-wifi.
When chaining Wireless tools, pair zong-wifi with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `zong-wifi --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
